name: youtube-transcript
description: Download and process YouTube video transcripts using yt-dlp. Use this when extracting subtitles, creating summaries from videos, or processing video content.
capabilities: Download YouTube video transcripts in multiple languages
Prioritize manually created subtitles over auto-generated ones
Convert VTT subtitle files to plain text
Handle videos without captions using Whisper fallback
author: Doyajin174
securitySummary: This skill consists solely of documentation describing yt-dlp usage patterns for downloading YouTube transcripts. All 38 static findings are false positives: cryptographic algorithm detections are random string false positives in JSON metadata; external command detections are documentation examples, not executable code; C2 keyword detection is a SHA256 hash being misidentified. The skill contains no executable code, no network operations beyond what yt-dlp performs, and no file access beyond standard Claude tool permissions.
valueStatement: Manually created captions often have better quality than auto-generated ones. This skill provides a systematic approach to download YouTube subtitles using yt-dlp with fallback options for videos without captions.
limitations: Requires yt-dlp to be installed on the system
Cannot extract transcripts from private or deleted videos

useCases.title: Research and reference
useCases.description: Extract transcripts from educational videos for quotes and references

useCases.title: Video summarization
useCases.description: Download captions to create summaries and chapter outlines from long videos

useCases.title: Multilingual content access
useCases.description: Get transcripts in native and target languages for study materials

promptTemplates.title: Basic transcript
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract the transcript from this YouTube video: [VIDEO_URL]

promptTemplates.title: Multi-language
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract both English and Korean subtitles from this video: [VIDEO_URL]

promptTemplates.title: Clean output
promptTemplates.prompt: Download the transcript and convert it to clean text without timestamps: [VIDEO_URL]

promptTemplates.title: Full processing
promptTemplates.prompt: Download the transcript, remove duplicates, create a summary with key topics and timestamps: [VIDEO_URL]

bestPractices: Prefer manual captions over auto-generated for better accuracy
Verify video availability before attempting download
Request user confirmation before using Whisper fallback for long videos
antiPatterns: Attempting to download from private or unavailable videos
Using auto-generated captions when manual ones are available
Processing large videos without checking file size first

faq.question: What languages are supported?
faq.answer: YouTube supports many languages. Use --list-subs to see available options for each video.

faq.question: What if a video has no captions?
faq.answer: Try Whisper fallback. This downloads the audio and uses OpenAI Whisper for transcription.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. Install yt-dlp first, then Claude can execute the transcript extraction workflow.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only accesses YouTube public data. No personal information is collected or stored.

faq.question: Why do auto-captions have duplicates?
faq.answer: Progressive captions on YouTube repeat content. The skill includes deduplication logic for cleaner output.

faq.question: How is this different from other transcript tools?
faq.answer: This skill prioritizes manually created captions for accuracy and provides systematic fallback options.
